justification: Brief is clearly scoped to Sprint 1 only. Sprint 2 retry logic is explicitly acknowledged and excluded ('do NOT implement retry logic, job queues, or BullMQ'). All three original ACs are present plus a bonus AC4 for POST format. 5-second timeout is included as a domain rule and architecture guidance. MJ's 'leave extension points' guidance is passed through with a pragmatic note about DeliveryResult containing enough info for future retry. No retry logic appears in implementation guidance.

observations.text: Task context immediately states Sprint 2 is deferred with explicit 'do NOT implement' instruction to Shaq
All three required ACs present (success, server error failure, timeout failure) plus a fourth for POST format
5-second timeout treated as domain constraint, not infrastructure default — called out in both domain_rules and architecture_guidance
Extension points guidance translated pragmatically: DeliveryResult carries enough info for future retry scheduler without scaffolding dead code
Architecture guidance explicitly includes 'No retry logic in Sprint 1' as a named decision with rationale
DeliveryResult value object design cleanly separates timeout/network errors (null http_status_code) from server errors
